channel.title: My Kata Life
channel.description: Recent content on My Kata Life

item.title: Karate: Green Belt!
item.description:
I’ve now reached Green Belt!

What did we learn?

Post on this coming soon

Tournament

In the process of earning this belt I competed in my first tournament. I performed H-Form #2. I also sparred in both regular as well as team sparring.

Team sparring was a lot of fun I definitely recommend it!

Techniques

  • Jump Front Kick / Mae Tobi Geri
  • Spinning Hook Kick / Ushiro Ura Mawashi Geri
  • Crescent Kick / Mikazuki Geri
    • Inside-Out Crescent Kick / Uchi Mikazuki Geri
    • Outside-In Crescent Kick / Soto Mikazuki Geri
  • Spinning Roundhouse
  • Spinning Backfist / Ushiro Uraken Uchi
  • Jab / Kizami-zuki
  • Hook Punch / Kagi-zuki
  • Back Ridge Hand / Ura Haito Uchi

Katas

We continued to work a lot on H-Form #1, H-Form #2 and H-Form #3. These were all on our test.

The new one this time for me was To-San. This was a really fun one to learn!

Next Belt!

Now we are ready to move on to the next belt. Next up Blue!

item.title: Karate: Orange Belt!
item.description:
I’ve now reached Orange Belt! On to Green!

What did we learn?

We learned more about martial arts and some more about other styles like:

  • Judo
  • Aikido
  • Jeet Kane Do

Read more here

Techniques

  • Outside Block/ Soto Uke
  • Inside Block/ Uchi Uke
  • Roundhouse / Mawashi Geri
  • Double Roundhouse / Nidan Mawashi Geri
  • Hook Kick / Ura Mawashi Geri
  • Knife Hand Strike / Shuto Uchi
  • Spinning Side Kick / Ushiro Mawashi Geri
  • Ridge Hand Strike / Haitō Uchi
  • High Check
  • Low Check

Katas

We worked a lot on H-Form #1 and the subsequent #2 and #3

Can find more about H-Form #1 here

Next Belt!

Now we are ready to move on to the next belt. Next up Green!

item.title: Karate: Yellow Belt!
item.description:
I’ve now reached Yellow Belt! I’m back to where I started 14 years ago!

What did we learn?

We learned a little bit about the history of Karate and what some of the terms used mean.

For example:

The term “karate” is derived from two Japanese characters: “kara” (空), meaning “empty,” and “te” (手), meaning “hand.” Together, they translate to “empty hand,” signifying a martial art that emphasizes unarmed combat

More can be found here

Techniques

  • Front Stance/zenkutsu-dachi
  • Down Block/gedan barai
  • Lunge Punch/oi-zuki
  • Double Lunge Punch/Nihon Zuki
  • Triple Lunge Punch/Sanbon Zuki
  • Ready Stance/Heiko-dachi
  • Fighting Stance
  • Back Fist Strike/Uraken Uchi
  • Fighting Punch/Choku-zuki
  • Front Kick/Mae Geri
  • Side Kick/Yoko Geri
  • Spinning Back Kick/Ushiro Geri

Katas

We started learning H-Form #1

Next Belt!

Now we are ready to move on to the next belt. Next up Orange!

item.title: My Journey Begins
item.description:
I’ve always been a fan of Martial arts. When I was a kid I wanted to start classes, but it never worked out for a number of reasons.

In college I finally had my first opportunity! I needed 1 PE credit for my degree and while looking through the course catalog and I found it. A karate class offered by Grand Master Chan Yu.

Yup thats me as an 18 year old white belt. Lets just say i’ve put a lot of meat on my bones since then.

I finished the class and got my yellow belt and was eager to keep going. So I went down to the dojo to sign up. When I got there and found out the cost I determined it was too expensive for my limited income. In hindsight… I believe they said something like $80 a session.. And my mental definition of a session was apparently $80 per class. 🤦

14 years later…

14 years almost to the day later..

My kids found out about a Karate class last year and we signed them up. They really enjoy the classes and we all like the instructors. They’ve already earned their orange belts.

My oldest kid needed to move to the adult class which had her going back from orange belt to white belt. The kids class move at slightly different rate. But to “keep motivated” dad joined. But in reality I wanted to anyways. After all these years of wanting to do Martial Arts I took my opportunity to get started. It’s a good way for us to bond and spend time together practicing, doing tournaments together etc.

I did start over as a white belt so i’m a bit behind my kids.. But, I don’t mind. Its all part of the journey.
